description: 30 year old female who's married & have 5 babies (cats).
prerequisites: Digital accessibility is such a crucial topic 🤝. To get started, let's cover some basics.  Accessibility concepts and terminology are essential. Some key terms include: - *A11y*: Short for accessibility - *Screen reader*: Software that reads web content aloud - *Alt text*: Alternative text for images - *ARIA*: Accessible Rich Internet Applications (for dynamic content)  W3C Accessibility Standards are guidelines for making web content more accessible. The main standards include: - *WCAG*: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines - *ATAG*: Authoring Tool Accessibility Guidelines - *UAAG*: User Agent Accessibility Guidelines  Basic knowledge of HTML and CSS is also necessary. Some key HTML elements for accessibility include: - *Semantic HTML* (e.g., `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`) - *Alt attributes* for images - *ARIA attributes* for dynamic content  What specific areas of digital accessibility do you want to focus on? Accessibility Is
